content:
# Emulsification

**Summary**: The formation of a stable mixture of two immiscible liquids.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2021-altowilib|Altowilib, 2021]] analyzed the stability of oil/water emulsions influenced by asphaltenes and resins.

- [[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024]] investigated emulsification as a method to control the reactivity of TCFs and improve their stability in diesel.

## Scientific Context

- [[2021-altowilib|Altowilib, 2021]] Emulsified oil causes operational issues in production and refining.

- [[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024]] investigated emulsification as a method to control the reactivity of TCFs and improve their stability in diesel.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2021-altowilib|Altowilib, 2021]] Evaluated via ESI over 20 minutes.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Asphaltenes and resins act as primary stabilizers for the interfacial film, increasing viscosity and hindering separation. ([[2021-altowilib|Altowilib, 2021, p. 26]])


- Emulsifier-A was found to be the most effective for creating stable water-in-oil (WO) emulsions with smaller droplet sizes. ([[2024-abdallah|Abdallah, 2024, p. 55]])
